messages.content:
India sold its government-owned aluminum mining company, Bharat Aluminum, to private investors in 2001. This is an example of
Group of answer choices

central planning.

differentiation.

an oligopoly.

trade tariffs.

privatization.
privatization.

When a country focuses on increasing employment, some increase in inflation is likely.


Group of answer choices

True

False
False.

To protect the American textile industries and workers, in 2004 the U.S. imposed a limit on the number of socks that can be imported from China. This is an example of a tariff.


Group of answer choices

True

False

False. 

This is an example of a quota, which is a limit on the quantity of a product that can be imported. Tariffs are taxes on imported goods, designed to make them more expensive compared to domestic products.

One of the most significant drawbacks of international mergers and acquisitions is the difficulty of integrating the operations, finances, and cultures of two very different organizations.



Group of answer choices

True

False
True.

Global trade became possible only after 1900.


Group of answer choices

True

False

False. 

Global trade has existed for centuries, even millennia. The Silk Road, for example, was a vast network of trade routes that connected China with the Mediterranean world as early as the 2nd century BC.

which of the following would be the best indicator of a country’s economic health?


Group of answer choices

High employment


High real GDP growth


Intense competition


A stable currency


Low inflation
High real GDP growth.

During the decade-long war in Iraq, the U.S. government spent two trillion dollars for military operations and reconstruction in that country. These costs are part of the U.S. government’s


Group of answer choices

price controls.



monetary policy. 


fiscal policy.


trade policy.



exchange rate policy.
fiscal policy.

If China maintains a tight monetary policy, what is the likely medium-term effect?


Group of answer choices

a. Higher inflation


b. More unstable currency


c. Higher unemployment


d. Lower interest rates


e. More consumer spending

N/A
a. Higher inflation.

Country A can produce soybeans and cotton more efficiently than Country B. Country A can produce 10 times more soybeans than Country B and 2 times more cotton. Country B can produce oil more efficiently than Country A and it can produce 5 times more oil than Country A. Which of the following is a true statement?



Group of answer choices

a. Country A has a comparative advantage in cotton and an absolute advantage in soybeans.


b. Country B has an absolute advantage in oil production.


c. Neither Country A nor Country B has an absolute advantage, but comparative advantages do exist.


d. Country B has an absolute advantage in soybeans and cotton.


e. Country A has a comparative advantage in cotton over soybeans.

b. Country B has an absolute advantage in oil production. 

c. Neither Country A nor Country B has an absolute advantage, but comparative advantages do exist. 

Explanation: 
- Country A has an absolute advantage in both soybeans and cotton, as it can produce more efficiently than Country B in both products.
- Country B has an absolute advantage in oil production, as it can produce more efficiently than Country A.
- However, when it comes to the concept of comparative advantage, it refers to the ability of a country to produce a good or service at a lower opportunity cost than another country. In this case, we don't have information about the opportunity cost, so we can only conclude that both countries have comparative advantages in different products. For example, even though Country A has an absolute advantage in soybeans production, it may still have a lower opportunity cost in producing cotton and vice versa.

When U.S.-based ExxonMobil ships refined oil in a tanker from Texas to Mexico, that is called __________. When ExxonMobil builds a refinery in Venezuela to process South American crude oil, that is __________.


Group of answer choices

a. foreign direct investment ; importing


b. exporting; importing


c. importing; foreign direct investment


d. exporting; foreign direct investment


e. importing; exporting
b. exporting; foreign direct investment.

General partnerships are able to spread liability over several owners, which results in “limited liability” for each partner.


Group of answer choices

True

False

False. 

General partnerships do not offer limited liability to any partner. Each partner is personally liable for the debts and obligations of the business.

A U.S. corporation’s decision to reincorporate in Bermuda may be both legal and unethical.


Group of answer choices

True

False
True.

Unethical behavior is always bad for corporate performance.


Group of answer choices

True

False

False. 

While unethical behavior is generally considered negative, there may be cases where unethical behavior leads to short-term gains for corporate performance. However, over the long run, unethical behavior may harm a company's reputation, stability, and performance.

As a proportion of total national employment, the U.S. has a _______ small business sector than ________.

Group of answer choices

a. larger; Canada. 

b. larger; many Asian countries


c. smaller; many Asian countries


d. larger; many European countries


e. smaller; many European countries
b. larger; many Asian countries.

What is the main difference between a C corporation and an S corporation?


Group of answer choices

a. A C corporation has double taxation of earnings and an S corporation doesn’t.


b. A C corporation has shareholders and an S corporation doesn’t.


c. A C corporation affords limited liability to its shareholders but an S corporation doesn’t. 


d. A C corporation is limited in size and an S corporation isn’t.


e. A C corporation does not dilute ownership control and an S corporation does.
a. A C corporation has double taxation of earnings and an S corporation doesn’t.
